intro: Crepinella umbellata, also known as Crepinella, is a plant species within the family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, with its leaves and stems possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial for soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Crepinella umbellata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Crepinella umbellata is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its health benefits, particularly in traditional folk medicine.
